<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rss version="2.0">
  <channel>
    <title>Manoj's Daily Essay Picks</title>
    <link>https://example.com/manoj-daily-essay-picks/</link>
    <description>Five serious articles and essays selected across science, ideas, culture, personal growth, politics, business, strategy and technology.</description>
    <language>en-us</language>
    <lastBuildDate>Mon, 13 Jul 2026 14:42:58 GMT</lastBuildDate>

    <item>
      <title>We Know Simple Fluids Can Flow. Turns Out, Some Can Fracture.</title>
      <link>https://www.quantamagazine.org/we-know-simple-fluids-can-flow-turns-out-some-can-fracture-20260710/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.quantamagazine.org/we-know-simple-fluids-can-flow-turns-out-some-can-fracture-20260710/</guid>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[A nonelastic liquid was observed cracking like a brittle solid, challenging a basic assumption about how fluids fracture.]]></description>
      <category>Science</category>
      <category>Physics</category>
      <category>Fluid Dynamics</category>
      <category>Materials Science</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>The Stretch of a Soul</title>
      <link>https://thepointmag.com/criticism/the-stretch-of-a-soul/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://thepointmag.com/criticism/the-stretch-of-a-soul/</guid>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[An exploration of biography, philosophy and whether the fullness of a human life can exceed intellectual explanation.]]></description>
      <category>Philosophy</category>
      <category>Personal Growth</category>
      <category>Culture</category>
      <category>Biography</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>B-Sides: Albert O. Hirschman’s “The Passions and the Interests”</title>
      <link>https://www.publicbooks.org/albert-o-hirschmans-the-passions-and-the-interests/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.publicbooks.org/albert-o-hirschmans-the-passions-and-the-interests/</guid>
      <pubDate>Sat, 04 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[Why capitalism was once defended as a way to tame destructive passions—and how prosperity can eventually threaten freedom.]]></description>
      <category>Business</category>
      <category>Political Economy</category>
      <category>Strategy</category>
      <category>History of Ideas</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>‘It Is Comforting to Be Haunted’: How Attitudes to Abortion Have Changed Through the Ages</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/jul/02/it-is-comforting-to-be-haunted-how-attitudes-to-abortion-have-changed-through-the-ages</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/jul/02/it-is-comforting-to-be-haunted-how-attitudes-to-abortion-have-changed-through-the-ages</guid>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Jul 2026 04:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[A personal and historical essay restoring bodily experience and circumstance to abstract arguments about abortion.]]></description>
      <category>Politics</category>
      <category>Society</category>
      <category>History</category>
      <category>Reproductive Rights</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Inside the Luddite Festival Harnessing Gen Z’s Rage Against Big Tech</title>
      <link>https://www.wired.com/story/inside-the-luddite-festival-harnessing-gen-zs-rage-against-big-tech/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.wired.com/story/inside-the-luddite-festival-harnessing-gen-zs-rage-against-big-tech/</guid>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Jul 2026 10:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[A grassroots experiment in replacing platform dependence with offline events, analog tools and community-owned infrastructure.]]></description>
      <category>Technology</category>
      <category>Culture</category>
      <category>Digital Life</category>
      <category>Society</category>
    </item>
  </channel>
</rss>

items.title: We Know Simple Fluids Can Flow. Turns Out, Some Can Fracture.
items.description: A nonelastic liquid was observed cracking like a brittle solid, challenging a basic assumption about how fluids fracture.

items.title: The Stretch of a Soul
items.description: An exploration of biography, philosophy and whether the fullness of a human life can exceed intellectual explanation.

items.title: B-Sides: Albert O. Hirschman’s “The Passions and the Interests”
items.description: Why capitalism was once defended as a way to tame destructive passions—and how prosperity can eventually threaten freedom.

items.title: ‘It Is Comforting to Be Haunted’: How Attitudes to Abortion Have Changed Through the Ages
items.description: A personal and historical essay restoring bodily experience and circumstance to abstract arguments about abortion.

items.title: Inside the Luddite Festival Harnessing Gen Z’s Rage Against Big Tech
items.description: A grassroots experiment in replacing platform dependence with offline events, analog tools and community-owned infrastructure.
